channel.title: What Is Being Built — Daily Activity Feed
channel.description: Daily updates on open-source automated research tools: commits, releases, and community activity.

item.title: Daily digest — 2026-06-22
item.description:
Our community has seen exciting progress across multiple projects recently. In the **scienceverse/metacheck** project, Lakens made significant updates to the `fix/code-check-paperlist` and `papers_databases` branches, fixing a crash and adding functions to load paper databases from **scienceverse/papers**. Meanwhile, the **giladfeldman/docpluck** project saw improvements to documentation and table fixes, and **shaheedazaad/auto-zcurve** revised its validation note with a new citation. Additionally, **giovannacdelsordo/output-it-forward** made it easier for users to access the OIF extension by adding a Chrome Webstore link. These updates demonstrate the connections and collaborations within our community, and we celebrate the progress being made across these open-source research tools.

106 commits across 6 active repositories this week.

New releases: metacheck v0.1.0-beta

Dormant repos revived: output-it-forward

Most active:

regcheck31 commits
docpluck30 commits
coarse30 commits
metacheck11 commits
escicheck3 commits

item.title: docpluck: 4 commits today
item.description:
giladfeldman/docpluck

The `feat/rc-t-table-region-guard` branch was worked on, where documentation was added for "RC-T Layer-2 + combined-ship closeout (cycle 2, v2.4.97)" in the learnings section. The `feat/rc-t-table-region-guard` branch also saw fixes to tables, specifically addressing "DP-2 blank p/df + DP-5 two-header-row recovery/super-header alignment + RC-T Layer-2 raw_text prose-trim/suppress" in version 2.4.97. Additionally, on the `feat/rc-t-table-region-guard` branch, a rendering fix was made to "strip Camelot tables that are absorbed body prose" in version 2.4.96, related to RC-T.

4 commit(s) today:

  • 3e9cc2c [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] docs(learnings): RC-T Layer-2 + combined-ship closeout (cycle 2, v2.4.97) — Gilad Feldman
  • 813aa4c [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] fix(tables): v2.4.97 — DP-2 blank p/df + DP-5 two-header-row recovery/super-header alignment + RC-T Layer-2 raw_text prose-trim/suppress — Gilad Feldman
  • 84a4d42 [feat/rc-t-table-region-guard] fix(render): RC-T — strip Camelot tables that are absorbed body prose (v2.4.96) — Gilad Feldman

View all commits →

item.title: regcheck: 1 commit today
item.description:
JamieCummins/regcheck

Work was done on the 'staging' branch, where security features were hardened. The 'qwen' feature was re-added, and subsequent commits focused on improving and firming up its formatting. Additionally, the logging was improved, and efforts were made to "hunt down" an OSF bug, which suggests that bug fixes were a focus. A push for redeploy was also made, indicating that changes were prepared for deployment. Furthermore, the repository was updated to address issues, with a specific commit mentioning "push for redeploy", implying that the changes were being prepared for a new deployment.

1 commit(s) today:

  • 71be3be [staging] harden security features — Jamie Cummins

View all commits →

item.title: escicheck: 1 commit today
item.description:
giladfeldman/escicheck

The main branch was the focus of the recent work, with two commits made to it. The first commit synced changes from escicheckapp@bd6f007, which corresponds to effectcheck version 0.6.5, as stated in the commit message. The second commit, also on the main branch, synced changes from escicheckapp@dec37b1, which is associated with effectcheck version 0.6.4. These commits brought the main branch up to date with the specified versions of effectcheck. The commits were made in close succession, with both occurring on the same day, June 21, 2026.

1 commit(s) today:

  • a588210 [main] sync from escicheckapp@bd6f007 (effectcheck v0.6.5) (#19) — Gilad Feldman
  • 1d48c4e [main] sync from escicheckapp@dec37b1 (effectcheck v0.6.4) (#18) — Gilad Feldman
  • 4be3331 [main] sync from escicheckapp@f52d7ce (effectcheck v0.6.3) (#17) — Gilad Feldman
  • 1f1a89a [main] sync from escicheckapp@e060e98 (effectcheck v0.6.2) (#16) — Gilad Feldman
  • f7e25f6 [sync/20260525-150441-b801333] sync from escicheckapp@b801333 (effectcheck v0.6.2) — github-actions[bot]
  • d0db89d [main] sync from escicheckapp@5093197 (effectcheck v0.6.1) (#14) — Gilad Feldman
  • a44f236 [main] sync from escicheckapp@1ebe05f (effectcheck v0.5.18) (#13) — Gilad Feldman

View all commits →

item.title: output-it-forward: activity update
item.description:
giovannacdelsordo/output-it-forward

The `main` branch was updated with a commit that added a Chrome Webstore link for OIF, as stated in the commit message "Add Chrome Webstore link for OIF". No other branches were worked on in this set of commits. This specific change involved adding a link, presumably to make it easier for users to access or install the OIF extension from the Chrome Webstore. The commit does not mention any other changes or additions beyond the Chrome Webstore link. There were no other commits to any other branches, so the rest of the repository remains unchanged.

⚠ This repository was dormant and has recently become active again.

View all commits →
